meaning: An abnormally high body temperature.
Expel air from the lungs with a sudden sharp sound.
A common viral infection of the nose and throat.
A highly contagious viral infection of the respiratory passages.
A condition marked by pain in the throat, typically caused by inflammation.
A condition in which excess fluid flows from the nostrils.
A continuous pain in the head.
A pain in a person's belly.
Pain in or around a tooth.
Pain inside the ear.
Prolonged pain in one's back.
Pain felt over the entire body.
A condition in which feces are discharged from the bowels frequently and in a liquid form.
A condition in which there is difficulty in emptying the bowels.
Pain or discomfort in the stomach associated with difficulty in digesting food.
A form of indigestion felt as a burning sensation in the chest.
A rash of round, red welts on the skin that itch intensely.
A respiratory condition marked by spasms in the bronchi of the lungs.
A disease in which the body's ability to produce or respond to the hormone insulin is impaired.
A medical condition in which the lens of the eye becomes progressively opaque.
A disease caused by an uncontrolled division of abnormal cells in a part of the body.
An infectious bacterial disease characterized by the growth of nodules in the tissues, especially the lungs.
An intermittent and remittent fever caused by a protozoan parasite.
A debilitating viral disease of the tropics, transmitted by mosquitoes.
An infectious bacterial fever with an eruption of red spots on the chest and abdomen.
An infectious and often fatal bacterial disease of the small intestine.
Lung inflammation caused by bacterial or viral infection.
Inflammation of the mucous membrane in the bronchial tubes.
Painful inflammation and stiffness of the joints.
Any disease marked by inflammation and pain in the joints, muscles, or fibrous tissue.
A medical condition in which the bones become brittle and fragile.
Abnormally high blood pressure.
Abnormally low blood pressure.
A sudden and sometimes fatal occurrence of coronary thrombosis.
A sudden disabling attack or loss of consciousness caused by an interruption in the flow of blood to the brain.
The loss of the ability to move in part or most of the body.
A neurological disorder marked by sudden recurrent episodes of sensory disturbance.
Progressive mental deterioration that can occur in middle or old age.
A progressive disease of the nervous system marked by tremor, muscular rigidity, and slow, imprecise movement.
A malignant progressive disease in which the bone marrow produces increased numbers of immature or abnormal leukocytes.
A condition marked by a deficiency of red blood cells or of hemoglobin in the blood.
A medical condition with yellowing of the skin or whites of the eyes.
A disease characterized by inflammation of the liver.
A chronic disease of the liver marked by degeneration of cells, inflammation, and fibrous thickening of tissue.
A medical condition in which the kidneys fail to adequately filter waste products from the blood.
A serious medical condition in which the appendix becomes inflamed and painful.
An open sore on an external or internal surface of the body, caused by a break in the skin or mucous membrane.
A condition in which part of an organ is displaced and protrudes through the wall of the cavity containing it.
A condition of increased pressure within the eyeball, causing gradual loss of sight.
An infectious viral disease causing fever and a red rash on the skin.
A contagious and infectious viral disease causing swelling of the parotid salivary glands in the face.
An infectious disease causing a mild fever and a rash of itchy inflamed blisters.
An eradicated virus that used to be contagious, disfiguring, and often deadly.
An infectious viral disease that affects the central nervous system and can cause temporary or permanent paralysis.
A contagious and fatal viral disease of dogs and other mammals that causes madness and convulsions.
A bacterial disease marked by rigidity and spasms of the voluntary muscles.
A contagious disease that affects the skin, mucous membranes, and nerves, causing discoloration and lumps on the skin.
A chronic, potentially life-threatening condition caused by the human immunodeficiency virus.
An acute respiratory illness in humans caused by a coronavirus.
Inflammation of the meninges caused by viral or bacterial infection.
Inflammation of the brain, caused by infection or an allergic reaction.
A life-threatening condition that arises when the body's response to infection causes injury to its own tissues and organs.
A chronic bacterial disease that is contracted chiefly by infection during sexual intercourse.
A venereal disease involving inflammatory discharge from the urethra or vagina.
A recurrent throbbing headache that typically affects one side of the head.
An acute, highly contagious bacterial disease causing inflammation of the mucous membranes.
Infection of the intestines resulting in severe diarrhea with the presence of blood and mucus in the feces.
A swelling of the neck resulting from enlargement of the thyroid gland.
A disease in which defective metabolism of uric acid causes arthritis.
A medical condition in which patches of skin become rough and inflamed.
A skin condition characterized by red pimples on the skin.
A damaging immune response by the body to a substance.
Wrench or twist the ligaments of an ankle, wrist, or other joint violently.
A stretching or tearing of a muscle or a tissue connecting muscle to bone.
The cracking or breaking of a hard object or material, typically a bone.
Injury or disability caused when the normal position of a joint or other part of the body is disturbed.
An injury appearing as an area of discolored skin on the body, caused by a blow or impact.
Make an opening, incision, or wound in something with a sharp-edged tool or object.
An injury to living tissue caused by a cut, blow, or other impact.
A deep cut or tear in skin or flesh.
A mark or wound made by scratching.
An injury or mark caused by scraping.
A superficial injury caused by rubbing against a rough surface.
An injury caused by exposure to heat or flame.
Injure with very hot liquid or steam.
Temporary unconsciousness caused by a blow to the head.
Injury caused by a severe jerk to the head, typically in a motor-vehicle accident.
Inflammation of the skin caused by overexposure to ultraviolet radiation from the sun.
Injury to body tissues caused by exposure to extreme cold.
A small, thin, sharp piece of wood, glass, or similar material broken off from a larger piece.
An abnormal enlargement of a part of the body, typically as a result of an accumulation of fluid.
The escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel, especially when external.
An escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel, especially when internal.
A mark left on the skin or within body tissue where a wound, burn, or sore has not healed completely.
A small bubble on the skin filled with serum and caused by friction, burning, or other damage.
A thickened and hardened part of the skin or soft tissue, especially in an area that has been subjected to friction.
A painful, involuntary contraction of a muscle or muscles.
A sudden involuntary muscular contraction or convulsive movement.
Breathing with a whistling or rattling sound in the chest.
Intense tightening in the chest, air hunger, difficulty breathing, breathlessness.
A noticeably rapid, strong, or irregular heartbeat due to agitation, exertion, or illness.
An involuntary quivering movement.
Exuding sweat as a result of being too hot, ill, or afraid.
The state of being numb; a lack of sensation.
Experience or cause to experience a slight prickling or stinging sensation.
A swollen state caused by retention of fluid or gas.
An involuntary spasm of the diaphragm and respiratory organs.
Habitual sleeplessness; inability to sleep.

A feeling of coldness occurring during a high fever.
Make a sudden involuntary expulsion of air from the nose and mouth due to irritation of one's nostrils.
The blockage of the nasal passages usually due to membranes lining the nose becoming swollen.
The thick viscous substance secreted by the mucous membranes of the respiratory passages.
A feeling of sickness with an inclination to vomit.
Eject matter from the stomach through the mouth.
An area of reddening of a person's skin, sometimes with raised spots.
Have or cause an uncomfortable sensation on the skin that makes one want to scratch.
A bluish discoloration of the skin resulting from poor circulation or inadequate oxygenation of the blood.
The condition of lacking the power of hearing or having impaired hearing.
The state or condition of being unable to see because of injury, disease, or a congenital condition.
An acutely disturbed state of mind that occurs in fever, intoxication, and other disorders.
A state of deep unconsciousness that lasts for a prolonged or indefinite period.
A sudden, violent, irregular movement of a limb or of the body.
A sudden attack of illness, especially a stroke or an epileptic fit.
Ringing or buzzing in the ears.
A sensation of whirling and loss of balance.
